title: Chamaemelum tanacetifolium
intro: Chamaemelum nana, also known as Tanacetum or Chamaemelum, and sometimes referred to as Pineapple Weed or Pineapple Chamomile, is a small, annual plant in the daisy family. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties, which may help to soothe skin irritations, reduce fever, and combat infections. The bioactive constituents of Chamaemelum include sesquiterpene lactones, chamazulene, and bisabolol, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Chamaemelum is commonly used to make herbal teas, salves, and infusions, as well as being incorporated into skincare products and aromatherapy blends due to its calming and soothing effects.
taxonomy: Chamaemelum tanacetifolium is classified as a species of flowering plant in the daisy family, Asteraceae. It belongs to the genus Chamaemelum, which consists of several species of low-growing, herbaceous plants. The specific epithet 'tanacetifolium' refers to the plant's resemblance to the tansy (Tanacetum vulgare) in terms of its foliage. Chamaemelum tanacetifolium is native to Europe and Western Asia. This plant is also known as Roman chamomile, but it is not to be confused with the true chamomile (Matricaria chamomilla).
uses: Chamaemelum nana and Chamaemelum nobile, however, Chamaemelum nana is also known as Chamaemelum tanacetifolium in some places, are both known for their medicinal uses. The plant has been used to treat a variety of ailments, including anxiety, insomnia, and skin conditions. Its essential oils have been used in aromatherapy to promote relaxation and reduce stress. Further research is needed to confirm the efficacy of Chamaemelum tanacetifolium's medicinal uses.

properties: Chamaemelum nobile, also known as Chamaemelum tanacetifolium, is sometimes confused with Chamaemelum tanacetifolium, however the correct common name for the plant species in question is actually Chamaemelum nobile. Chamaemelum nobile has been used in traditional herbal medicine for its therapeutic properties. It is believed to have anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antispasmodic effects. constituents: Chamaemelum nobile's relative is Chamaemelum nobile not Chamaemelum tanacetifolium, but Chamaemelum nobile's relative has been found to contain various compounds. parts: Chamaemelum tanacetifolium, also known as Tansy, is a plant with a long history of use in traditional medicine. preparations: Chamaemelum tanacetifolium, commonly known as Roman chamomile, has been used in herbal preparations for its calming and soothing properties. It is often used to treat anxiety, insomnia, and restlessness due to its sedative effects. The plant's essential oil, which is extracted from its flowers, is a key ingredient in many herbal remedies.
